## 🔴 URGENT — Act This Week

### 1. Sport Outlet (NO) — Dual vacancy: CTO + CDO
**Signal type:** Leadership vacuum
**What happened:** Both CTO and CDO roles are vacant as of March 2026. Neither replacement has been announced.
**Why it matters:** Vacancy = no internal champion defending the status quo. CEO Tor-André Skeie is the only decision-maker in the room. Budget is unallocated until the new hires arrive.
**GTM match:** Commerce Optimization
**Entry offer:** Commerce Optimization Pilot — €40–60K
**Suggested action:** Cold outreach to Tor-André Skeie this week. Frame as "before the new CTO/CDO sets the agenda."
**Estimated deal:** €150–250K
**Window:** Closing. New hires could appear any week.

---

### 2. Trumf / NorgesGruppen (NO) — New Commercial Director, day ~75
**Signal type:** New executive — 90-day window
**What happened:** Rikke Etholm-Idsøe appointed to newly created Commercial Director role at Trumf AS.
**Why it matters:** New role = no predecessor, no established vendor relationships, agenda still being shaped. She is building her commercial programme. Trumf's own leadership admitted retail media is "not big enough" — she was likely hired to fix that.
**GTM match:** Data Revenue Unlock
**Entry offer:** Data Revenue Diagnostic — €60–80K
**Named buyers:** Rikke Etholm-Idsøe (Commercial Dir.) + Kristin Bigseth (MD, Trumf AS)
**Suggested action:** LinkedIn outreach to Rikke — already drafted. Send today.
**Estimated deal:** €450K
**Window:** ~15 days remaining in peak honeymoon phase.

### 3. Siteimprove (DK) — New CMO, day 6
**Signal type:** New executive — tightest window
**What happened:** Jen Jones appointed CMO at Siteimprove. Started 3 March 2026.
**Why it matters:** SaaS CMO in first two weeks = evaluating every growth channel and partner. Hello Growth fits exactly. Window closes fast as she builds relationships.
**GTM match:** Hello Growth (SaaS track)
**Entry offer:** Hello Growth — DKK 200–250K fixed fee
**Named buyer:** Jen Jones (CMO)
**Suggested action:** LinkedIn Version B — send today. This is the most time-critical outreach in the entire pipeline.
**Estimated deal:** DKK 250K (~€34K)
**Window:** ~20 days remaining before she's settled in.

## 🟡 HIGH — Act This Month

### 4. Vinmonopolet (NO) — New CDO, honeymoon phase
**Signal type:** New executive
**What happened:** Espen Terland appointed CDO at Vinmonopolet. Comes from 15 years at XXL — exactly the kind of retail data background that recognises the value of a diagnostic immediately.
**Why it matters:** He knows what good product data architecture looks like from his XXL experience. He will be assessing every system and vendor in the first 90 days. JAKALA's entry point is the conversation about what comes next — not what's broken.
**GTM match:** Data Revenue Unlock → Commerce Optimization
**Entry offer:** Data Revenue Diagnostic — €50–80K
**Named buyer:** Espen Terland (CDO) + Lars Thorenfeldt (Dir. Category & Digital)
**Suggested action:** Draft LinkedIn outreach this week. Lead with his XXL background — frame as "you know what best-in-class data infrastructure looks like."
**Estimated deal:** €180K
**Window:** ~60 days.

### 5. H&M Group (SE) — Google Cloud AI partnership live
**Signal type:** AI initiative + macro tailwind
**What happened:** H&M Group has an active Google Cloud AI partnership. Sweden's National AI Strategy (SEK 12.5B investment, Feb 2026) provides macro backdrop. H&M speaking at eCommerce Expo 2026 on product information.
**Why it matters:** ICP 9 · Deal 9 · €900K unweighted — the largest single opportunity in the Nordic pipeline. The Google Cloud partnership confirms AI investment intent. Product information as a conference topic signals internal awareness of the data quality challenge.
**GTM match:** Data Revenue Unlock → AI Readiness
**Entry offer:** Data Revenue Diagnostic — €75–100K
**Named buyer:** TBD — Global Product Information Lead (referenced at eCommerce Expo 2026 speaker list)
**Suggested action:** Identify the Product Information Lead from H&M's eCommerce Expo speaker list. One name unlocks €225K weighted value immediately.
**Estimated deal:** €900K
**Blocker:** No named buyer — capped at 25% win probability until identified.

---

### 6. Clas Ohlson (SE) — DC automation + 20% ecommerce growth
**Signal type:** Infrastructure investment + commercial signal
**What happened:** Clas Ohlson is investing MSEK 400–450 in distribution centre automation (starting March 2026). Ecommerce grew 20% YoY. Acquisitions: Phonelife, Reservdelaronline.
**Why it matters:** When logistics investment accelerates, digital catalog performance must keep pace. Automated DC means faster fulfilment — but only if product data is accurate enough to support it. Helena Holmström (CMO) is confirmed buyer.
**GTM match:** Commerce Optimization → Data Revenue Unlock
**Entry offer:** Commerce Optimization Pilot — €40–70K
**Named buyer:** Helena Holmström (CMO)
**Suggested action:** Outreach to Helena — frame around "your logistics investment needs a product data foundation that can keep pace."
**Estimated deal:** €350K

---

### 7. Matas Group (DK) — SymphonyAI selected January 2026
**Signal type:** AI vendor selection — adjacent layers open
**What happened:** Matas selected SymphonyAI for category management AI in January 2026. Partnership announced publicly.
**Why it matters:** AI for category management is the beginning, not the end. Personalization, product discovery, and recommendation layers are all adjacent — and all require clean product data as input. SymphonyAI doesn't cover these. ICP 9 · Deal 9 · €700K.
**GTM match:** AI Readiness Accelerator → Commerce Optimization
**Entry offer:** AI Readiness Diagnostic — €50–80K ("what comes after category AI")
**Named buyer:** TBD — CTO / CDO (buyer gap is the only blocker)
**Suggested action:** LinkedIn research to identify Matas CTO or CDO this week. One name = €175K weighted added to forecast.
**Estimated deal:** €700K
**Blocker:** No named buyer.
